mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free way to run Kubernetes without managing the underlying hardware. Key trade-offs include reliance on Linode's infrastructure and potential costs associated with managed services.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This installs the Linode CLI on your local machine.
commands: curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-east

```

> Adjust the region as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

```

> This saves the kubeconfig to your local machine.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary RBAC and service account configurations.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster and all associated resources.

uninstall.title: Remove Kubeconfig
uninstall.description:
This command removes the kubeconfig file from your local machine.

```bash

rm ~/.kube/config

```

> This will delete your local kubeconfig file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify the cluster is functioning correctly.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command: linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy a sample application to verify the cluster is functioning.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient nodes available to fulfill the request'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count using: linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --node-count <new-count>

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is accessible from the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features such as PodSecurity admission controller and improved resource quotas.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy network plugins.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure to review the new resource quota configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming that your cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with your API token.
